mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the unemployment rate signals tightening labor markets that boost consumer spending power and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.3 percentage points within 30 days
failure_condition: The signal produces negative or flat 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts and residential fixed investment indicates robust aggregate demand expansion, which lifts corporate earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 prices higher over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 1.5% over any 3-year rolling window, or the cross-sectional correlation between the two proxies drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle demonstrates that real economic output is outpacing financial friction, allowing equities to sustain valuation premiums and generate positive forward returns.
failure_condition: Industrial production growth correlates negatively with S&P 500 20-day returns in tightening periods, or the hypothesis yields a negative Sharpe ratio over any 12-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating quarterly real GDP growth expands the earnings base for S&P 500 constituents, mechanically driving positive index returns over the subsequent week as analysts revise forecasts upward.
failure_condition: The rolling Spearman correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and next-week SPX returns remains below 0.05 across all available vintage data.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month consumer price index increase exceeding 0.4% triggers inflation hedging demands and anticipates aggressive central bank rate hikes, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal generates positive 20-day forward returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or inflation surprises fail to move real yields in the expected direction.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and lifting equity valuations over the following quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive information coefficient relative to a baseline GDP-only model, or forward returns in triggered periods do not exceed the unconditional mean by at least 100 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production output alongside stable federal funds rates indicates healthy capacity utilization without financial overheating, supporting corporate margin expansion and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The hypothesis yields a negative predictive R-squared in a 12-month rolling regression, or SPX forward returns show no statistical difference between high/low INDPRO quartiles when FEDFUNDS is stable.
